name: Charley
title: Chair
text:
Originally from England UK, they have been calling North Leitrim home since 2013. 
Charley is Bi/Pan and is a founding member of Leitrim Pride and has worked with several other LGBTQ+ groups down the years including OutWest and Sligo Pride, she is a current Steering Group member of Full Spectrum Ireland (Ireland's first disabled LGBTQI+ peer support group). 
Her passion for promoting visibility and acceptance for the LGBTQ+ community can also be seen in their professional work, as she is the co-owner of Tully Crafts with their mother Davina, which sells a wide range of LGBTQ+ and Esoteric hand crafted items, currently stocking over 40 different LGBTQ+ flag designs. They can often be found making items for Tully Crafts of an evening.

name: Niall
title: Secretary
text:
Niall is originally from Sligo and has lived in South-Leitrim for the last 20 years. 
He has worked as YES Equality Leitrim Co-Lead (2015) during the 2015 Marriage Equality Referendum. He helped set-up 8 Rays Leitrim – Leitrim’s first LGBTI+ community and social group (2011-present). 
He currently serves as secretary of Leitrim Pride Festival and is one of the founding members of Sligo Pride Festival (2019-2021). In 2020, he joined the steering group of FULL SPECTRUM IRELAND: Disabled LGBTI+ Peer Network as the acting-chair. 
Niall is trained as a sexual health promoter with the Foundational Programme in Sexual Health Promotion (FPSHP) and HIV Ireland’s ‘Let’s Talk About... Safer Sex!” training for trainers programme. 
Key Interests: LGBTI+ Rights, Disability Rights, Sexual Health and Well-being, Mental Health

name: Andrea
title: Treasurer
text:
Andrea is coming up to her second year of life in Leitrim, having been happily blown off course from Brighton. 
She was part of an LGBT+ parent's group there, which met weekly in order to share and support each other so that they could in turn support teens. She volunteered at Transpride Brighton where she was blown away at seeing so many young people happy and comfortable in their skins, just being themselves. 
She believes in the importance promoting the visibility for the LBGT+ community and that is what Leitrim Pride does. 
Andrea has worked in the finance sector of various women's groups in Dublin and London for many years.

name: Isolde
title: Events Chair
text:
Isolde was born and reared in Dublin, but escaped in 1999, and has now lived in Leitrim longer than she ever did in the big schmoke. 
She works as a wheelchair dancer with Undercurrent dance company, facilitates an online disabled writers’ group, and has fingers in many other artistic pies. Her alter-ego, Ms Honeysuckle Rose, can be spotted at Leitrim Pride events. 
Her voluntary work has always been rights-based, from co-founding Full Spectrum Ireland (peer network for disabled LGBTI+ people in Ireland), to campaigning for Marriage Equality and Together for Yes, to various Pride festivals. 
She does most of this work from her living room, in the company of her guide-dog Ari and (currently) four cats. She runs on tea.

name: Marty
title: OCM
text:
Marty (he/him) was born in England, and has lived in Co. Leitrim since 1986. 
Having worked in England for fifteen years teaching second level sciences, and then in Ireland in Community Development and Adult Education, Marty is now retired from paid work. 
Marty has been involved with OutWest since 2002, originally as a patron attending their social meetings, before joining their Committee in 2004. 
Marty is also on the Board of LGBT Ireland which, among other things, oversees the National LGBT Helpline. Marty was actually involved in the initial planning sessions before the launch of the Helpline in 2010.

name: Katie
title: OCM
text:
Katie identifies as a Bisexual woman, she is originally from England UK and moved to Ireland in 2003. She has been living in south Leitrim for just over 2 years. 
She originally joined Leitrim pride as a steering committee member in the Summer of 2021 and has continued to participate as an Ordinary Committee Member since then.
Katie has previously done voluntary work with LINC, a group dedicated to advocating for lesbian and bisexual woman in Ireland. 
Katie was keen to join the committee as she felt there needed to be more representation of the LGBTQ+ community, especially in rural Ireland.
